Passed enforced test review-ldiskfs-dne on RHEL 8.10 / x86_64 uploaded by Trevis Autotest from trevis-35vm1: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/fbb78e6e-2a46-45d0-8a14-9ed8b0730f7d ran 5 tests.

Patch Set 1:

Chris you are the author of this code. Is this change okay?

Patch Set 1:

This change is fine, but I think it would be good to also modify jt_reset_fault() to enforce specifying -t argument. The segfault occurs because the opc is not set. -t should be mandatory argument and user should be told as much if it is not specified.

Patch Set 1:

It would also be good to have tests that check that both reset does not segfault and that it actually resets stats.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 30868de94003ae436d7177b92e22553c22f54b9f by Oleg Drokin

commit.subject: LU-18751 lnet: Segfault in lnetctl fault command
commit.message:
LU-18751 lnet: Segfault in lnetctl fault command

"lnetctl fault reset 0" and similar variations cause a segfault. This
is caused by a null pointer that is not checked in the code.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Frank Sehr <fsehr@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Iec580b19f97c2a189ae8f29444bf3e3cc91d78a0
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/58216
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Serguei Smirnov <ssmirnov@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Manish Regmi <mregmi@ddn.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
